title: oncostylis paradoxa
intro: Oncostylis paradoxa, also known as the 'stinging tree' or 'Dendrocnide moroides', is a plant native to Australia and Papua New Guinea, characterized by its highly toxic and irritant sap. The plant's medicinal properties have been utilized in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Oncostylis paradoxa include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, although its use is often limited due to its high toxicity. In herbal preparations, the sap of the plant is typically dried and processed into a powder or extract, which can be used to create topical creams and ointments.
